# KBC Financial Operations Update

*Building a clearer, more accountable, and more sustainable system*

> We are not trying to replace one person with another person. We are trying to build a financial system that is clear, accountable, transferable, and sustainable.

| Why This Matters | What We Are Trying to Fix |
| --- | --- |
| The Treasurer transition gives KBC an opportunity to strengthen the system.<br>Clear financial practices help protect trust and support good stewardship.<br>Better documentation makes the work easier to review and hand off. | The Treasurer role has become broad and difficult for one person to carry.<br>Routine bookkeeping and officer-level review need clearer separation.<br>Some financial processes need clearer written steps and committee review. |

| Proposed Direction | How Work Will Be Divided |
| --- | --- |
| The Treasurer remains an accountable elected officer.<br>The congregation authorized a paid, part-time Bookkeeper position on June 28, 2026.<br>The goal now is to make the job description, duties, controls, and hiring process clear. | Treasurer: accountability, review, reporting, and Finance Committee coordination.<br>Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator: records, reimbursements, reports, deposits, and documentation.<br>Finance Committee: controls, software, budget review, reimbursements, and monthly reporting.<br>Personnel Committee: job description, application, hiring, compensation, and evaluation process.<br>Nominating Committee: coordination as named in the motion. |

| What Comes Next | What the Church Is Being Asked to Support |
| --- | --- |
| Personnel Committee will finalize the job description, application, and hiring process.<br>Finance Committee will confirm financial duties, access, controls, software needs, and budget impact.<br>Additional items requiring church approval will come back to the church. | A careful hiring process within the authority already given.<br>Clearer responsibilities and better documentation.<br>Good stewardship, accountability, and sustainability.<br>Future church votes where bylaws, budget, policy, or church practice require them. |
